content.problemStatement:
You have 222 positive integers XXX and YYY.

In one operation, you can change the value of **one** of the integers by 111 or ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.

Find the minimum number of operations needed to ensure that either X‚Č•(2‚čÖY)X \ge (2 \cdot Y)X‚Č•(2‚čÖY) or Y‚Č•(2‚čÖX)Y \ge (2 \cdot X)Y‚Č•(2‚čÖX), i.e. either XXX is at least twice as large as YYY, or YYY is at least twice as large as XXX.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of test case contains 222 space-separated integers XXX and YYY.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of operations.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Y‚Č•(2‚čÖX)Y \ge (2 \cdot X)Y‚Č•(2‚čÖX) already holds as 4‚Č•(2‚čÖ2)4 \ge (2 \cdot 2)4‚Č•(2‚čÖ2).
Test case 222: Decrease YYY twice by 111 to get X=4,Y=2X = 4, Y = 2X=4,Y=2. Then, X‚Č•(2‚čÖY)X \geq (2\cdot Y)X‚Č•(2‚čÖY) holds.
This is the minimum possible number of operations.
Test case 333: Increase YYY by 111 to get X=3,Y=6X = 3, Y = 6X=3,Y=6. Then Y‚Č•(2‚čÖX)Y \ge (2 \cdot X)Y‚Č•(2‚čÖX) holds.
